apiOrSchemaChanges: New schema: Capabilities { maxTextLength: integer|null, supportsInstructions: boolean, supportsStreaming: boolean, outputFormats: [string] }
/providers.json and /api/v1/voices.json responses gain capabilities blocks per record
failingTest: No explicit test yet — this is a feature request. The "failure" is that downstream consumers cannot programmatically discover per-provider/voice capabilities, limits, or the job status enum from the catalog contract.
fileMap: openapi.yaml (or openapi.json) — modify — add Capabilities schema, SynthesisJobStatus enum, extend Provider/Voice schemas
Sources/.../Provider.swift (or equivalent model) — modify — add capabilities property to provider model
Sources/.../Voice.swift (or equivalent model) — modify — add capabilities property to voice model
Sources/.../provider-catalog-handler (or equivalent) — modify — include capabilities in /providers.json response
Sources/.../voice-catalog-handler (or equivalent) — modify — include capabilities in /api/v1/voices.json response
Tests/.../CatalogTests (or equivalent) — modify — add assertions for capabilities presence and shape
Sources/.../provider-data (seed/config) — modify — annotate each provider with its capabilities
fixApproach: Add a new `Capabilities` schema object (maxTextLength, supportsInstructions, supportsStreaming, outputFormats) and a `SynthesisJobStatus` enum to the OpenAPI spec. Extend the Provider and Voice schemas with an optional `capabilities` field. Populate capabilities data in the catalog endpoints (/providers.json, /api/v1/voices.json) and the underlying data layer. This is purely additive — no existing fields change, no breaking API changes.
openQuestions: What language/framework is Vokda built with? (affects file paths and test command)
Where exactly is the OpenAPI spec located in the repo?
What are the exact status values for the synthesis job enum? (need to inventory current code)
Are provider capabilities hardcoded seed data, config-driven, or introspected live from provider APIs?
Should voice capabilities inherit from provider by default, or be independently specified? What override semantics?
What are the real maxTextLength values for each provider? (4096 for OpenAI is noted; others need research)
Is there a staging or integration environment to validate the catalog changes before merging?
steps: 1. Identify and read the existing OpenAPI spec file, Provider model, Voice model, catalog handler(s), and any seed/static data files to understand current shapes.
2. Design the Capabilities schema: { maxTextLength: integer|null, supportsInstructions: boolean, supportsStreaming: boolean, outputFormats: [string] } and the SynthesisJobStatus enum values.
3. Add the Capabilities schema and SynthesisJobStatus enum to the OpenAPI spec; extend Provider and Voice components/schemas with optional `capabilities: Capabilities`.
4. Update the Provider model (struct/class) to include an optional `capabilities` property matching the schema.
5. Update the Voice model (struct/class) to include an optional `capabilities` property; decide inheritance rule (voice inherits provider unless overridden).
6. Populate capabilities data in provider seed/config — at minimum for `openai`, and for every other active provider.
7. Update the /providers.json catalog handler to serialize capabilities in the response.
8. Update the /api/v1/voices.json catalog handler to serialize capabilities (inherited or per-voice) in the response.
9. Add or update tests: verify each provider/voice response includes a well-formed capabilities block, verify the OpenAPI spec validates, verify status enum values round-trip.
10. Run the full test suite and confirm no regressions.
summary: Add a capabilities + limits block to each provider and voice record in the public catalog and OpenAPI schemas, plus document the synthesis job status enum.
